data.title: Sixt: Depressed Valuation and Strong U.S. Used-Car Pricing Create Compelling Upside
data.description:
Jefferies analyst Constantin Hesse maintained a Buy rating on Sixt SE today and set a price target of €110.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Constantin Hesse has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Sixt’s earnings backdrop and market valuation. Robust U.S. used‑car pricing, as reflected in the Manheim index reaching its strongest level since mid‑2023, supports higher residual values, easing depreciation risk and boosting returns on fleet disposals in the first quarter.

At the same time, Sixt’s share performance has significantly lagged peers like Avis and Hertz, despite stronger operating metrics, leaving the stock trading at an unusually steep discount of roughly 60% to Avis. With a sizeable portion of Sixt’s mainly U.S. risk fleet directly benefiting from this favorable pricing environment, Hesse views the current valuation as unduly depressed and sees compelling upside potential for the shares.

In another report released on March 31, Deutsche Bank also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a €85.00 price target.

data.title: Positioning Ivonescimab to Anchor a ~$200B PD-1xVEGF Market: Early NSCLC Validation and Commercial Head Start Support Buy Rating on Summit
data.description:
Stephen Willey, an analyst from Stifel Nicolaus, has initiated a new Buy rating on Summit Therapeutics (SMMT).

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Stephen Willey has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to ivonescimab’s position and data within the emerging PD-1xVEGF class. He views Summit’s lead asset as well placed to anchor what he projects could be a roughly $200 billion market, with its cooperative binding design and comparatively mature dataset supporting the potential for superior immunotherapy efficacy and a durable competitive moat.

He also emphasizes that early clinical readouts already show compelling progression-free survival and a favorable overall-survival trend versus Keytruda, which he believes materially de-risk Summit’s first-line NSCLC phase 3 program. In his view, this early validation, combined with Summit’s multi-year commercial head start, sizeable peak-opportunity in lung cancer, and the prospect of broader indication expansion, supports meaningful upside to the current valuation and underpins his Buy recommendation.

Willey covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Disc Medicine, Exelixis, and Incyte. According to TipRanks, Willey has an average return of 7.3% and a 41.98% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on April 2, Wells Fargo also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $30.00 price target.

data.title: Leveraging Merck–Terns Benchmark: Supporting Enliven’s ELVN-001 Upside and a Raised $60 Buy Target
data.description:
LifeSci Capital analyst Sam Slutsky maintained a Buy rating on Enliven Therapeutics today and set a price target of $60.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sam Slutsky has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to recent transaction benchmarks and comparative clinical data. He notes that Merck’s multi‑billion‑dollar acquisition of Terns establishes a robust valuation reference point for next‑generation chronic myeloid leukemia agents, even after Merck reduced its initial bid in response to updated TERN‑701 trial results, which it still viewed as competitively compelling versus asciminib.

Slutsky then contrasts TERN‑701’s efficacy profile with Enliven’s ELVN‑001, emphasizing that while he expects TERN‑701’s MMR rate to settle in the low‑50% range, ELVN‑001 is likely to deliver around a 40% MMR rate in a tougher patient population, which he believes is attractive in light of Merck’s pricing of Terns. Combined with Enliven’s solid cash runway of roughly 3.3 years and a meaningful short interest that could support upside if sentiment improves, these factors underpin his decision to maintain a Buy rating and raise the price target to $60 per share.

Slutsky covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Palvella Therapeutics, Enliven Therapeutics, and Olema Pharmaceuticals. According to TipRanks, Slutsky has an average return of 40.2% and a 51.74% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released yesterday, JonesTrading also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $45.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: AstraZeneca: Oncology and CVRM Momentum, Pipeline Progress, and FX Tailwinds Underpin Raised £165 Price Target and Buy Rating
data.description:
In a report released today, Sarita Kapila from Morgan Stanley reiterated a Buy rating on AstraZeneca, with a price target of p16,500.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sarita Kapila has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including expectations that AstraZeneca’s first-quarter 2026 sales will slightly exceed market forecasts, with particularly strong contributions from the Oncology and CVRM franchises. She highlights continuing robust momentum from key oncology assets such as Imfinzi, Calquence, and Enhertu, as well as outperformance from Farxiga in the cardiovascular and metabolic segment.

In addition, her earnings projections for Q1’26 are modestly above consensus, and she anticipates management will reaffirm guidance for solid mid- to high-single-digit revenue growth and low double-digit core EPS expansion in 2026. Incorporating favorable currency effects and recent positive pipeline news, including advances for tozorakimab in COPD and efzimfotase alfa in HPP, she raises the base-case price target to £165 per share and maintains a positive risk-reward view that supports the Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, J.P. Morgan also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a £160.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: SuRo Capital: Upgraded Fundamentals, Strategic Magnetar Partnership, and Deep NAV Discount Support Buy Rating
data.description:
Analyst Alexander Paris of Barrington reiterated a Buy rating on SuRo Capital, boosting the price target to $17.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Alexander Paris has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, starting with SuRo Capital’s sharp improvement in fundamentals. The company’s estimated first-quarter NAV per share has surged into the mid-teens from just above $8 at year-end, helped by higher valuations in key holdings such as OpenAI and Whoop!, and the shares still trade materially below this updated NAV range.

He also views the planned shift to an external management model with Magnetar as a strategic catalyst that should enhance deal sourcing, deepen technology and AI ecosystem expertise, and lower recurring costs through a more efficient fee structure. Combined with a robust private financing environment, anticipated IPO monetizations in 2026, and a sizable discount to a conservatively set $17 target price, these elements support his expectation of meaningful upside in the stock.

In another report released today, TipRanks – Google also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $12.50 price target.
